OG Anunoby since his age-24 season (5 seasons, age-24 through 28 seasons): 16.8 ppg, 4.9 rpg, 2.2 apg, 1.6 spg, 47.4 FG%, 37.8 3P%, 80.6 FT%, 23.8 win shares

Those numbers sure do look like Yaxel Lendeborg numbers

items.title: As of right now, Austin Reaves will check in as the 16th highest-paid guard in the NBA next season. He's just behind Darius Garland & Ja Morant and just ahead of LaMelo Ball & Tyrese Maxey.
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
As of right now, Austin Reaves will check in as the 16th highest-paid guard in the NBA next season. He's just behind Darius Garland & Ja Morant and just ahead of LaMelo Ball & Tyrese Maxey.

SGA is also just behind Reaves. This is the final season of his deal before his super max extension kicks in.

items.body_excerpt: Bryson crushed it last night. Caleb Wilson was a no-brainer, but I thought Swain was an inspired choice. items.title: I know some are shocked at the starting value of this deal, but it's important to remember that it's all relative as a percentage of the cap:
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
I know some are shocked at the starting value of this deal, but it's important to remember that it's all relative as a percentage of the cap:

Austin Reaves is getting 25% of the cap in Year 1. That's the lowest maximum contract possible, because of his years of service.

items.title: Having a 4th year player option for an older "young" guy in Reaves is good stuff. He can see extension money sooner/hit free agency earlier. I think with the 140% extension rule, agents are really trying to ensure their clients access more money faster.
items.author: Shamit Dua
items.body_excerpt: Having a 4th year player option for an older "young" guy in Reaves is good stuff. He can see extension money sooner/hit free agency earlier. I think with the 140% extension rule, agents are really trying to ensure their clients access more money faster.

items.title: Sparks say Kelsey Plum is out with a lower left leg injury. She will be re-evaluated in four weeks. Kelsey Plum was clearly hurt during their last game. She kept adding tape to that leg every quarter and was limping heavily postgame. Hopefully, this time out will get her body bac
items.author: Edwin Garcia
items.body_excerpt: Sparks say Kelsey Plum is out with a lower left leg injury. She will be re-evaluated in four weeks. Kelsey Plum was clearly hurt during their last game. She kept adding tape to that leg every quarter and was limping heavily postgame. Hopefully, this time out will get her body back to full health.

items.title: The four-week timeline puts Plum’s reevaluation date around July 22, which means the 31-year-old is slated to miss at least the next 10 games. The Sparks, who are 8-8, are looking to make the playoffs for the first time since 2020.
items.author: Justin Russo
items.body_excerpt: The four-week timeline puts Plum’s reevaluation date around July 22, which means the 31-year-old is slated to miss at least the next 10 games. The Sparks, who are 8-8, are looking to make the playoffs for the first time since 2020.

items.title: New: Mike Gansey opened the door for the Sixers to "be aggressive" trading into tonight's second round of the 2026 NBA Draft.
items.author: Adam Aaronson
items.body_excerpt:
New: Mike Gansey opened the door for the Sixers to "be aggressive" trading into tonight's second round of the 2026 NBA Draft.

So, a primer: what Gansey said, which future second-rounders the Sixers have at their disposal and trade partners and prospects to watch: www.phillyvoice.com/sixers-news-...

media.title: Mike Gansey is ready to 'be aggressive' during second round of 2026 NBA Draft. The Sixers have pathways to trading in.
media.description: As he discussed Sixers No. 22 overall pick Labaron Philon Jr., Mike Gansey opened the door for some second-round action during the 2026 NBA Draft.

items.title: Of note, Kingston Flemings projects to make about $7.35 million in Year 1 of his rookie-scale contract.
items.author: Brad Rowland
items.body_excerpt:
Of note, Kingston Flemings projects to make about $7.35 million in Year 1 of his rookie-scale contract. 

Zuby Ejiofor projects at about $3.45 million in Year 1.

So slightly less than $11 million total to add to the Hawks cap sheet, which was already the expectation.

items.title: FWIW -- I wouldn't mind seeing Atlanta trade a future second rounder for Ugonna Onyenso.
items.author: Brad Rowland
items.body_excerpt:
FWIW -- I wouldn't mind seeing Atlanta trade a future second rounder for Ugonna Onyenso.  

Think he's probably the best rim protector in the entire draft other than Mara.

Obviously real limitations elsewhere, but can see him being a backup center in the NBA for a long time.
